refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit connection to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, optimization logic, and operator awareness. This supports both direct operation and teleoperation modes.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_consumed / total_tonnage_moved over a defined evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour shift, multi-shift campaign). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-reference with load/distance data. Conduct acceptance testing under representative worksite conditions with Site Manager and Equipment Operator oversight.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion and auxiliary systems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log work output (tonnage moved, distance traveled, or equivalent productivity metric).
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display real-time energy_per_ton ratio to operators and tele-operators.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide control recommendations or automated optimization to minimize energy_per_ton without compromising safety or task completion.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency metrics to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality for post-shift analysis and continuous improvement.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no optimization feedback)
DP-B: Active energy optimization with real-time feedback loop (measure → compute efficiency → recommend/automate control adjustments → feedback to operator)
DP-C: Hybrid approach with operator-selectable efficiency modes (eco, standard, performance) that adjust drivetrain parameters and provide mode-specific feedback
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Active energy optimization with real-time feedback loop. The NGHE solution shall implement a closed-loop energy management system that continuously measures power draw and work output, computes energy_per_ton in real-time, and provides actionable feedback (visual/auditory alerts, control recommendations, or automated drivetrain adjustments) to operators and tele-operators to maintain efficiency ≤ E_target. The system shall log all metrics for post-shift analysis.

potential_sources: Energy measurement (power sensors) is independent of work output measurement (load cells, odometry) — low coupling.
Real-time computation of efficiency ratio is decoupled from control recommendation logic — can be implemented separately.
Feedback delivery (UI, alerts) is decoupled from optimization algorithm — supports both direct and tele-operation modes.
Logging and reporting are decoupled from real-time feedback — can use asynchronous data pipeline.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits low coupling across measurement, computation, feedback, and logging domains. Each sub-function can be designed and tested independently, then integrated via well-defined data interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking). This supports modular implementation and reduces risk of cascading failures.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Energy sensors (power meters, current sensors) are available on Vehicle and can be read with sufficient accuracy and latency (<1 second) to enable real-time feedback.
ASSUMPTION: Work output metrics (load, distance, tonnage) can be reliably measured or inferred from Vehicle telemetry.
ASSUMPTION: Operators and tele-operators will respond to efficiency feedback and adjust behavior accordingly; system does not assume autonomous optimization alone will meet target.
RISK: If sensor accuracy is poor or latency is high (>5 seconds), real-time feedback may be misleading and operators may make suboptimal decisions.
RISK: If optimization algorithm is overly aggressive (e.g., recommends unsafe speed reductions), operators may override feedback, reducing effectiveness.
RISK: Tele-operator latency (network round-trip) may delay feedback delivery, reducing responsiveness compared to direct operation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly specify: (1) sensor accuracy and latency requirements; (2) feedback prioritization (safety-critical alerts override efficiency recommendations); (3) fallback behavior if sensors fail (revert to passive monitoring); (4) tele-operator feedback delivery mechanism (e.g., dashboard update frequency, alert thresholds). Consider adding a safety gate: if any safety-critical system detects a hazard, efficiency optimization shall be suspended and control returned to safe defaults.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. For CR-NGHE-003, the NGHE Solution IM owns the energy efficiency KPI orchestration: it coordinates measurement data from Vehicle (power, load, distance), computation of energy_per_ton, feedback delivery via Teleoperation (to operators and tele-operators), and logging for Maintenance/Quality. The NGHE Solution IM does not directly implement sensors or control actuators (those are Vehicle responsibilities) but owns the cross-module energy management logic and KPI accountability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives real-time telemetry from Vehicle: power consumption (kW), load (tons), distance (m), speed (m/s), operational state. Used to compute energy_per_ton and detect efficiency anomalies.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution sends control recommendations or mode adjustments to Vehicle via Tasking interface (e.g., 'reduce speed to 5 m/s', 'switch to eco mode', 'optimize bucket fill pattern'). Operator or tele-operator may accept or override.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts from Safety Services (e.g., 'proximity hazard detected', 'stability margin low'). If safety alert is active, NGHE Solution suspends efficiency optimization and prioritizes safe operation. NGHE Solution may also send efficiency-related alerts to Safety Services for logging (e.g., 'energy target exceeded, investigate fuel consumption').

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to deliver real-time efficiency feedback to Teleoperation module (for tele-operator dashboard) and to transmit logged metrics to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality systems. Latency and bandwidth constraints apply.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of cross-module energy management orchestration. It owns the KPI definition, computation logic, feedback prioritization, and logging. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry and Tasking interfaces cross module boundaries. Safety coordination via SafetyAlert is well-defined. High containment reflects that energy efficiency is a system-level concern, not a Vehicle-only or Teleoperation-only concern.

Vehicle could own energy measurement and some optimization (e.g., drivetrain efficiency tuning), but energy_per_ton KPI computation and operator feedback delivery require cross-module coordination. If Vehicle owned the full DP, it would need to know about operator preferences, tele-operator feedback, and safety constraints — high diffusion. Better to keep Vehicle focused on power/load measurement and control execution, leaving KPI orchestration to NGHE Solution.

Teleoperation could own feedback delivery to tele-operators, but it should not own energy measurement or KPI computation (those are Vehicle and NGHE Solution responsibilities). If Teleoperation owned the full DP, it would duplicate measurement logic and create inconsistency between direct and tele-operation modes. Better to keep Teleoperation as a feedback delivery channel, not a computation owner.

Safety Services should not own energy efficiency KPI. However, Safety Services must be aware of efficiency-related decisions that could impact safety (e.g., if efficiency optimization recommends aggressive speed reduction, Safety Services must validate that it does not create new hazards). Coordination via SafetyAlert interface is appropriate; ownership by Safety Services is not.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the optimal owner of CR-NGHE-003 DP because: (1) Energy efficiency is a system-level KPI that requires integration of Vehicle measurement, Teleoperation feedback, and Safety Services constraints. (2) NGHE Solution can own the computation and orchestration logic without duplicating measurement or control logic across modules. (3) Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimize diffusion. (4) Ownership by NGHE Solution enables consistent KPI tracking across direct and tele-operation modes.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution does not have real-time access to Vehicle telemetry (e.g., due to network latency or sensor failures), energy_per_ton computation will be delayed or inaccurate, reducing feedback effectiveness.

RISK: If Tasking interface does not support fine-grained control recommendations (e.g., only supports on/off commands, not continuous adjustments), NGHE Solution cannot implement smooth optimization and may resort to coarse feedback that operators ignore.

RISK: If Safety Services does not provide timely SafetyAlert signals, NGHE Solution may recommend efficiency optimizations that conflict with safety constraints, creating operator confusion or safety violations.
RISK: If Network Link bandwidth is insufficient for real-time tele-operator feedback, tele-operators will not receive timely efficiency information and cannot adjust behavior accordingly.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit SLAs for each interface: (1) Telemetry: latency <1 second, accuracy ±5% for power and load. (2) Tasking: support for continuous control parameters (speed, bucket angle, etc.), not just discrete commands. (3) SafetyAlert: latency <500 ms for critical alerts. (4) Network Link: minimum bandwidth for real-time dashboard updates (e.g., 10 Hz for efficiency metrics). Conduct interface design review with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services teams to confirm feasibility. Define fallback behavior if any interface degrades (e.g., revert to passive monitoring if Telemetry latency exceeds 5 seconds).

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-003 clearly specifies the outcome (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) and the mechanisms (measurement, computation, feedback, logging). Selected DP-B (active energy optimization with real-time feedback loop) directly addresses all sub-FRs: measurement (CR-NGHE-003.1, 003.2), computation (CR-NGHE-003.3), optimization (CR-NGHE-003.4), and reporting (CR-NGHE-003.5). DP-B is neither over-specified (does not prescribe specific sensor types or algorithms) nor under-specified (clearly defines the feedback loop structure). Coupling check passes: measurement, computation, feedback, and logging are decoupled. FR-DP mapping is sound.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): Energy measurement logic is owned by Vehicle, not NGHE Solution. However, this is appropriate: Vehicle owns sensors and raw data, NGHE Solution owns KPI computation. Interface (Telemetry) is well-defined.

DIFFUSION RISK (Low): Feedback delivery to tele-operators is owned by Teleoperation module, not NGHE Solution. However, NGHE Solution owns the feedback content and prioritization logic. Interface (Network Link) is well-defined.

DIFFUSION RISK (Low): Safety coordination is owned by Safety Services, not NGHE Solution. However, NGHE Solution must respect SafetyAlert signals. Interface (SafetyAlert) is well-defined and bidirectional.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment score is 95/100, indicating strong ownership of the DP. Diffusion is minimal and well-managed via defined interfaces. No evidence of algorithm duplication, logic fragmentation, or unclear ownership boundaries. Recommended: confirm that NGHE Solution has sufficient computational resources (CPU, memory) to run real-time energy_per_ton computation and feedback logic without impacting other system functions.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-003 is verifiable via acceptance testing: measure energy_per_ton over a defined evaluation period and confirm ≤ E_target. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of Vehicle power and load sensors; (2) NGHE Solution logging of energy and work metrics; (3) Site Manager oversight of test execution; (4) post-test analysis by Maintenance/Quality. Feasibility is high if interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are implemented as specified. Recommended: define acceptance test plan and success criteria in parallel with detailed design.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-003 to NGHE Solution IM is clear. Sub-FRs are allocated as follows: CR-NGHE-003.1 (measurement) → Vehicle (owns sensors) + NGHE Solution (owns logging); CR-NGHE-003.2 (work output measurement) → Vehicle (owns sensors) + NGHE Solution (owns logging); CR-NGHE-003.3 (real-time display) → NGHE Solution (owns computation) + Teleoperation (owns UI); CR-NGHE-003.4 (optimization) → NGHE Solution (owns logic) + Vehicle (owns control execution); CR-NGHE-003.5 (reporting) → NGHE Solution (owns data) + Maintenance/Quality (owns analysis). Allocation is traceable and non-overlapping.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to include explicit safety gates: if SafetyAlert is active, suspend efficiency optimization and revert to safe defaults. Document this in the DP specification.
Establish interface SLAs (latency, accuracy, bandwidth) for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link. Conduct interface design review with all module owners.
Define fallback behavior for NGHE Solution if any interface degrades (e.g., if Telemetry latency exceeds 5 seconds, revert to passive monitoring and alert operator).
Confirm that NGHE Solution has sufficient computational resources for real-time energy_per_ton computation. If not, consider offloading to a dedicated energy management controller or edge device.
Define acceptance test plan and success criteria for CR-NGHE-003 in parallel with detailed design. Include test scenarios for direct operation, tele-operation, and safety-critical conditions.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 refined FR is not atomic: it decomposes into five sub-FRs (measurement, work output, real-time display, optimization, reporting). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define detailed DPs and IMs. For example, CR-NGHE-003.4 (optimization) requires specification of optimization algorithms, control parameters, and feedback prioritization. CR-NGHE-003.3 (real-time display) requires specification of UI elements, alert thresholds, and tele-operator dashboard design. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to achieve atomic FRs and detailed design specifications. Current level (system-level energy efficiency KPI) is appropriate for gate review and allocation, but further decomposition is required for implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion and auxiliary systems. [Decompose into: sensor selection, data acquisition, logging format, data retention]

CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log work output (tonnage moved, distance traveled, or equivalent productivity metric). [Decompose into: sensor selection, data acquisition, logging format, data retention]

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display real-time energy_per_ton ratio to operators and tele-operators. [Decompose into: computation algorithm, UI design, alert thresholds, feedback delivery mechanism]

CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide control recommendations or automated optimization to minimize energy_per_ton without compromising safety or task completion. [Decompose into: optimization algorithm, control parameter selection, safety constraints, operator override mechanism]

CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency metrics to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality for post-shift analysis and continuous improvement. [Decompose into: data aggregation, report format, delivery mechanism, analysis tools]

revise.why: FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment are sound. No revisions needed before proceeding to next decomposition level. However, recommended revisions from gate review (interface SLAs, safety gates, fallback behavior, computational resources, acceptance test plan) should be incorporated into detailed design.

assumptions: Energy sensors (power meters, current sensors) are available on Vehicle and can be read with sufficient accuracy (±5%) and latency (<1 second).
Work output metrics (load, distance, tonnage) can be reliably measured or inferred from Vehicle telemetry.
Operators and tele-operators will respond to efficiency feedback and adjust behavior accordingly.
Tasking interface supports fine-grained control recommendations (continuous parameters, not just discrete commands).
Safety Services provides timely SafetyAlert signals (latency <500 ms for critical alerts).
Network Link has sufficient bandwidth for real-time tele-operator feedback (e.g., 10 Hz for efficiency metrics).
NGHE Solution has sufficient computational resources for real-time energy_per_ton computation.
E_target (customer-defined energy efficiency threshold) is specified and agreed upon before detailed design.
open_risks: RISK: Sensor accuracy or latency may be insufficient for real-time feedback. Mitigation: conduct sensor evaluation and interface SLA review early in detailed design.
RISK: Optimization algorithm may be overly aggressive and conflict with safety constraints. Mitigation: implement safety gates and operator override mechanism.
RISK: Tele-operator latency may delay feedback delivery. Mitigation: design feedback mechanism to tolerate network latency (e.g., predictive feedback, local caching).
RISK: Operators may ignore efficiency feedback if it conflicts with task completion or comfort. Mitigation: conduct human factors study and refine feedback design based on operator feedback.

RISK: Energy efficiency KPI may not be achievable with current Vehicle design. Mitigation: conduct feasibility study and define fallback acceptance criteria (e.g., 'best effort' if E_target is not achievable).
evidence_needed: Sensor specifications and accuracy data for power, load, and distance measurement.
Interface latency and bandwidth measurements for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Computational resource requirements for real-time energy_per_ton computation (CPU, memory, power).
Operator feedback on efficiency feedback design (UI, alert thresholds, recommendation clarity).
Feasibility study: can current Vehicle design achieve E_target? If not, what design changes are required?
Safety analysis: what are the potential safety impacts of efficiency optimization? How are they mitigated?
Acceptance test plan and success criteria for CR-NGHE-003.
